 {"id":"bd-gfd4","title":"Installer: remove set -e and echo -e","description":"install.sh currently uses global 'set -euo pipefail' and relies on 'echo -e' for colored logging; AGENTS.md requests no global set -e and best practice prefers printf. Make install.sh explicit-error-handling (set -uo pipefail), switch logging to printf, and avoid Bash-4-only lowercase expansions in prompts for broader portability.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-04T19:43:24.045649031-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T19:45:32.845850452-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T19:45:32.845850452-05:00","close_reason":"Remove global set -e, switch logging to printf, add explicit error handling, avoid bash-4-only lowercase expansion"}
 {"id":"bd-gokv","title":"E2E: Error handling and recovery tests","description":"## Objective
End-to-end tests for error conditions and recovery mechanisms.

## Test Scenarios
1. Network timeout handling
2. Disk space exhaustion
3. Permission denied scenarios
4. Corrupted state file recovery
5. Interrupted operation resume
6. Concurrent access conflicts

## Requirements
- Simulate realistic failure conditions
- JSON logging: error_type, context, recovery_action, success
- Verify graceful degradation
- Test cleanup after failures

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 6 scenarios pass
- [ ] Errors produce actionable messages
- [ ] Recovery mechanisms restore valid state
- [ ] No data loss on recoverable failures","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T21:57:13.668603363-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T21:57:13.668603363-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-gokv","depends_on_id":"bd-6crg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T21:57:26.497279804-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-gokv","depends_on_id":"bd-g7gw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T21:57:41.06728777-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-gptu","title":"Implement rate-limit governor (adaptive concurrency)","description":"Task: Implement Rate-Limit Governor

Purpose
-------
Dynamically adjust parallelism based on real rate limit data from GitHub
API and model responses, preventing throttling and cascading failures.

Background: Why Adaptive?
-------------------------
Static parallelism is dangerous:
- Too high: hit rate limits, get blocked
- Too low: waste time when limits are fine
- Limits vary by time, account tier, prior usage

Solution: query real limits, adjust dynamically.

Governor State
--------------
GOVERNOR_STATE associative array:
- github_remaining: remaining API calls
- github_reset: when limit resets (epoch)
- model_in_backoff: currently backing off?
- model_backoff_until: when backoff ends
- effective_parallelism: current allowed sessions
- circuit_breaker_open: emergency stop?

Implementation
--------------

start_rate_limit_governor()
  Run in background during review:
  while review lock held:
    update_github_rate_limit
    check_model_rate_limit
    adjust_parallelism
    sleep 30

update_github_rate_limit()
  Query: gh api rate_limit
  Extract: .resources.core.remaining and .reset
  If remaining < 500: log warning, reduce parallelism

check_model_rate_limit()
  Scan session logs for 429/rate limit patterns
  If multiple recent hits:
    Set model_in_backoff = true
    Set backoff_until = now + 60 seconds

adjust_parallelism()
  Start with target = REVIEW_PARALLEL (default 4)
  
  If github_remaining < 1000: target /= 2
  If model_in_backoff: target = 1
  
  Count recent errors in sliding window
  If errors > 5 in 5 minutes:
    Open circuit breaker
    target = 0
    Log emergency pause
  
  Set effective_parallelism = target

can_start_new_session()
  Return false if:
  - circuit_breaker_open
  - model_in_backoff
  - active_sessions >= effective_parallelism
  Otherwise true

Circuit Breaker
---------------
Pattern for handling cascading failures:
- CLOSED: normal operation
- OPEN: too many failures, pause all
- HALF-OPEN: after cooldown, try one (not implemented yet)

Metrics Tracked
---------------
- API calls per minute
- 429 responses per session
- Session error rate
- Effective vs requested parallelism

Integration Points
------------------
- Called before starting new session
- Called in session monitor loop
- Updates TUI status display
- Writes metrics for analytics

Testing
-------
- Mock gh api rate_limit responses
- Simulate 429 responses
- Verify parallelism adjusts
- Verify circuit breaker triggers

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Queries real GitHub rate limits
- [ ] Detects model rate limits from logs
- [ ] Adjusts parallelism dynamically
- [ ] Circuit breaker prevents cascading failures
- [ ] Metrics tracked for analysis","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:38:47.005795803-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T19:18:06.99800939-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T19:18:06.99800939-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented rate-limit governor with: GOVERNOR_STATE tracking, GitHub/model rate limit detection, circuit breaker pattern, adaptive parallelism. 13 unit tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-gptu","depends_on_id":"bd-jm89","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T16:17:00.313011296-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-hmw8","title":"Implement command validation and blocking","description":"Security: Validate commands before execution in agent sessions.

Three categories:

SAFE_BASH_COMMANDS (always allowed):
  git, grep, find, ls, cat, make, npm, cargo, go, python, pytest,
  shellcheck, eslint, prettier, jq, sed, awk, sort, diff

APPROVAL_REQUIRED_COMMANDS (human confirmation):
  rm, mv, cp (file operations)
  curl, wget (network)
  docker, kubectl (containers)

BLOCKED_COMMANDS (never allowed):
  sudo, su, chmod +x, chown, eval, exec

Special gh handling:
  READ allowed: gh issue view, gh pr view, gh issue list
  BLOCKED in Plan mode: gh issue comment, gh issue close, gh pr merge

validate_agent_command() checks command against lists, returns:
  0 = allowed
  1 = blocked
  2 = needs approval

Called by pre-exec hook in agent sessions.

Acceptance: Blocked commands rejected, approval commands queued, gh mutations blocked in Plan mode.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:43:25.152161697-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T17:02:09.080859708-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T17:02:09.080859708-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented command validation with 19 passing tests","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hmw8","depends_on_id":"bd-9s7y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:17.523931567-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-i1jw","title":"Real unit tests for URL parsing functions","description":"Test URL parsing without mocks using real string operations.

Functions to test:
- parse_repo_url(): Handle git@, https://, ssh:// URLs
- parse_repo_spec(): Parse owner/repo, full URLs, local paths
- normalize_url(): Standardize URL formats
- url_to_local_path(): Convert URLs to filesystem paths
- url_to_clone_target(): Get clone target from URL

Test cases:
- All URL schemes (https, git@, ssh)
- Edge cases (trailing slashes, .git suffix)
- Invalid URLs (proper error handling)
- Local path handling

No mocks needed - pure string manipulation tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T21:53:35.099743032-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T23:05:59.886908371-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T23:05:59.886908371-05:00","close_reason":"Added 30 unit tests for normalize_url, url_to_local_path, and url_to_clone_target. All 76 tests pass (156 assertions).","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-i1jw","depends_on_id":"bd-fudb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T21:53:35.143522928-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-i6mc","title":"Fix state lock eval + unsafe RU_STATE_DIR","description":"acquire_state_lock used eval to open lock fd; write_json_atomic used echo; and several review-state paths accepted relative/unsafe RU_STATE_DIR/XDG_STATE_HOME which could create directories under CWD. Remove eval, use printf, and harden path selection to absolute/tilde paths only.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-04T19:55:28.783651473-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T19:55:46.79692854-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T19:55:46.79692854-05:00","close_reason":"Removed eval from acquire_state_lock (uses exec {fd}); switched write_json_atomic to printf; normalized state-dir base selection to avoid relative/CWD writes"}
